title: The Nuanced Relationship Between Public Opinion and Foreign Policy

Three Council of Councils experts in France, India, and Nigeria reflect on how public opinion does—and sometimes does not—affect foreign policy decisions.

title: American Crisis Management for a New Era
summary: The United States lacks a rigorous, routinized approach to managing foreign policy crises. The Wachenheim Center for Peace and Security has developed a set of Crisis Response Playbooks and a Crisis Management Checklist to serve as quick-reference guides for policy planners during the immediate pre-crisis and active crisis phases, when bandwidth is limited and decisions are most consequential. title: Trump, Taiwan, and the Reagan Doctrine
summary: Every few months, critics proclaim that Donald Trump has “gone soft” on China over Taiwan. Their latest evidence is an alleged pause in a second major arms package ahead of Xi Jinping’s expected visit to Washington this fall—it is “alleged” because the U.S. government has never officially announced, let alone notified Taipei of such a pause.

title: Anja Manuel: Aspen Security Forum 2026 | Day 3
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Anja Manuel is part of a panel discussion concerning the shifting balance of power in the Indo-Pacific, rising regional tensions, the future of US alliances and partnerships, and the diplomatic, military, and economic tools needed to preserve stability.

summary: Hoover Institution fellow Anja Manuel has a wide-ranging conversation on US trade policy, economic security, strategic competition, and the growing intersection between national security and the global economy.

title: Mamdani Is ‘Not Even Making Any Effort To Disguise His Prejudices’ | Victor Davis Hanson

title: Trump threatens 'major military punishment' after Houthi attacks on tankers in Red Sea
summary: International oil prices topped $100 a barrel after Houthi rebels in Yemen claimed they attacked two oil tankers in the Red Sea, raising new fears of disruptions to global energy supplies. In response, President Trump warned that if the Houthis attack another ship, the U.S. would inflict "major military punishment." He also warned of escalation inside Iran. Nick Schifrin reports.

title: Former U.S. diplomat breaks down Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping
summary: For perspective on the Houthis' efforts to block shipping in the Red Sea, Amna Nawaz spoke with Timothy Lenderking. During his decades-long career as a U.S. diplomat, he served as special envoy to Yemen and as acting assistant secretary for Near East Affairs. He's now at the law firm Squire Patton Boggs.

title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool
summary: Chinese policy around the use of their SPR as a geoeconomic tool, particularly in crisis scenarios, is not well understood. This project investigates potential ways for China to do so, but not whether China actually intends to use it in this way.

title: Slowing Data Center Construction Signals the Need for Pro-Growth Reforms

title: June's Inflation Relief Was a Peace Dividend
summary: American families finally saw some financial relief in June as prices at the pump fell. Those lower fuel prices pulled down the consumer price index (CPI) by 0.4% in June, the largest monthly decline since April 2020. But don’t pop the champagne corks just yet, because that improvement has started reversing this month. Alas, June’s lower prices weren’t the triumph of sound monetary or fiscal policy, but a peace...

title: Living with Wet Basements
summary: This report presents findings from a study conducted in Pittsburgh to document the prevalence of and experiences with basement wetness, the barriers that residents face in addressing it, and options available to solve the problem.

title: Examining the Impact of Senate Bill 1160 on Utilization Review and Medical Treatment in California Workers' Compensation
summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Securing AI Algorithmic Insights
summary: In this report, the authors extend a security framework to provide the first systematic roadmap for protecting algorithmic insights, the novel techniques, methods, and design know-how that materially improve artificial intelligence systems.

title: AllHere Chatbot Scandal Shows How Not to Deploy AI

The episode is a case study of the need for greater transparency in public schools and the dangers...

Door honderden miljarden dollars aan subsidies, een ondergewaardeerde munt en een enorme exportgroei overspoelt China steeds meer buitenlandse...

title: Unpacking Europe: The path to EU enlargement
summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement. title: Coast Guard Polar Security Cutter (PSC) and Arctic Security Cutter (ASC) Icebreaker Programs: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The size of the Coast Guard’s polar icebreaker fleet and Coast Guard programs for building new polar icebreakers have been oversight matters for Congress for many years. A 2023 Coast Guard fleet mix analysis concluded that the service will require a total of eight to nine polar icebreakers, including four to five heavy polar icebreakers and four to five medium polar icebreakers, to perform the Coast Guard’s polar...

title: Navy Force Structure and Shipbuilding Plans: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The current and future size and composition of the Navy, the annual rate of Navy ship procurement, the prospective affordability of the Navy’s shipbuilding plans, the capacity of the U.S. shipbuilding industry to execute the Navy’s shipbuilding plans, and Navy proposals for retiring existing ships have been oversight matters for the congressional defense committees for many years. Congressional focus on these...

title: Navy Constellation (FFG-62) and FF(X) Class Frigate Programs: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: Constellation (FFG-62) class program. The Navy began procuring Constellation (FFG-62) class frigates in FY2020. Prior to November 2025, Navy plans called for procuring a total of at least 20. Six were procured through FY2025, including one per year in FY2020-FY2023, two in FY2024, and none in FY2025. The Navy’s proposed FY2026 budget did not request the procurement of any additional FFG-62s. FFG-62s are being built...

title: Navy Aegis Ballistic Missile Defense (BMD) Program: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: The Aegis ballistic missile defense (BMD) program, which is carried out by the Missile Defense Agency (MDA) and the Navy, gives Navy Aegis cruisers and destroyers a capability for conducting BMD operations. BMD-capable Aegis ships operate in European waters to defend Europe from potential ballistic missile attacks from countries such as Iran, and in in the Western Pacific and the Persian Gulf to provide regional...

title: Navy Large Unmanned Surface Vessels (USVs): Background and Issues for Congress
summary: Among the Navy’s programs for acquiring unmanned surface vessels (USVs) of various sizes are programs for acquiring large USVs that would be about the size of a large patrol craft, and which would deploy directly from pier rather than from the deck of a crewed Navy warship. Navy plans call for equipping large USVs with weapons and/or sensors or other electronic payloads and operating them in conjunction with crewed...
